content:
First observe that the elements of M in increasing order are

M₁=1, M₂=2, M₃=4, M₄=5, M₅=7, M₆=8,… 

in fact one checks easily that

 if k is odd,  k=2j−1,  then  M_k=3(j−1)+1=(3k−1)/2,  
 if k is even, k=2j,   then  M_k=3(j−1)+2=(3k−2)/2.  

Hence for any i and any N=2n,

 ∑_{k=i}^{i+2n−1}M_k 
  = ∑_{k=i}^{i+2n−1}\Bigl(\tfrac32\,k - e_k\Bigr)
  = \tfrac32·\frac{(i+(i+2n−1))·2n}2 \;-\;\sum_{k=i}^{i+2n−1}e_k
  =3n(i+n-1)\,,

where one checks that ∑e_k=3n/2.  Setting this equal to 300 gives

 3n(i+n−1)=300  ⟹  n(i+n−1)=100.  

Letting b=i+n−1 this says n·b=100 with b≥n (so that i=b−n+1≥1).  Thus n is a positive divisor of 100 with n≤b=100/n, i.e. n²≤100.  The divisors of 100 not exceeding 10 are

 n=1,2,4,5,10.

One checks in each case that i=b−n+1 is a positive integer, so all five work.  

Answer:  \boxed{n\in\{1,2,4,5,10\}}.

M₁=1, M₂=2, M₃=4, M₄=5, M₅=7, M₆=8,…

 if k is even, k=2j,   then  M_k=3(j−1)+2=(3k−2)/2.

 ∑_{k=i}^{i+2n−1}M_k

where one checks that ∑e_k=3n/2.  Setting this equal to 300 gives
